scope:
1. ACCOUNT OPERATIONS
To operate, manage, and conduct transactions on the designated bank accounts including deposits, withdrawals, and internal transfers between the designated accounts listed herein.
2. PAYMENT ORDERS & WIRE TRANSFERS
To issue payment instructions and authorise wire transfers from the designated accounts subject to the following financial limits:
— Single transaction limit: AED 500,000 (Five Hundred Thousand UAE Dirhams)
— Monthly cumulative limit: AED 3,000,000 (Three Million UAE Dirhams)
— Transactions exceeding these limits require dual signatory authorisation with the General Manager.
3. CHEQUE OPERATIONS
To sign, issue, and deposit cheques drawn on the designated AED and USD current accounts. Cheque value limit per instrument: AED 250,000. Blank cheque issuance is strictly prohibited.
4. TRADE FINANCE
To apply for, accept, and manage Letters of Credit (LC), Bank Guarantees (BG), and Documentary Collections through Mashreq Bank account MSQ-00387291-007. Maximum LC/BG value: AED 1,500,000 per instrument.
5. BANKING CORRESPONDENCE & DOCUMENTATION
To sign bank forms, account maintenance requests, mandate updates, and official banking correspondence. To collect bank statements, certificates of balance, and bank confirmation letters on behalf of the Company.
6. ONLINE BANKING ACCESS
To access and operate the Company's corporate internet banking platforms for the designated accounts for executing transactions within the limits specified herein. The Grantee shall not modify security credentials or add new beneficiaries without prior written approval from the General Manager.
7. FOREIGN EXCHANGE
To execute foreign exchange transactions and currency conversions on behalf of the Company through the designated bank accounts, subject to a single FX transaction limit of USD 150,000 or equivalent.
Limitations:
EXCLUSIONS: This POA expressly does NOT authorise the Grantee to: (a) open new bank accounts or close existing accounts; (b) apply for loans, overdraft facilities, or any credit products; (c) pledge or encumber Company assets as security; (d) sub-delegate banking authority to any third party; (e) execute transactions for personal benefit or any purpose unrelated to the Company's business operations.
issuingAuthority: UAE Federal Law — Central Bank of UAE Regulations

scope:
Numbered points:
1. GOVERNMENT & REGULATORY REPRESENTATION
To represent, appear before, and deal with all UAE federal and emirate-level government authorities, ministries, departments, municipalities, free zone authorities, and regulatory bodies on all matters relating to the Company's trade licence, commercial registration, permits, approvals, and filings. Includes: DED, Dubai Land Department, Dubai Courts, MOHRE, ICA, GDRFA, and all equivalent authorities.
2. CONTRACT EXECUTION
To negotiate, sign, execute, amend, renew, and terminate contracts, agreements, and legally binding documents on behalf of the Company. Contract value threshold under this authority: AED 2,000,000 (Two Million UAE Dirhams) per transaction. Transactions exceeding this limit require prior Board approval.
3. EMPLOYMENT & LABOUR MATTERS
To sign employment contracts, process visa applications and cancellations, submit labour filings to MOHRE, apply for and renew work permits, and represent the Company before the Ministry of Human Resources and Emiratisation.
4. CUSTOMS & TRADE
To represent the Company before UAE Customs, Dubai Customs, and Federal Customs Authority for import, export, and re-export transactions. To sign customs declarations and clearance documentation.
5. LEGAL PROCEEDINGS
To appoint legal counsel, file and defend lawsuits, and appear before courts, arbitration tribunals, and dispute resolution bodies. This authority does NOT include settlement of disputes exceeding AED 500,000 without prior Board approval.
6. COMPANY ADMINISTRATION
To manage the Company's official correspondence, collect company documents and certificates from government bodies, and manage trade licence renewals, amendments, and related filings.
Limitations:
This POA does NOT confer authority to: (a) dispose of, mortgage, or encumber any real property owned by the Company; (b) incur financial obligations exceeding the thresholds specified herein without Board approval; (c) sub-delegate this POA to any third party; (d) execute transactions contrary to applicable UAE federal or local laws.
issuingAuthority: UAE Federal Law — Laws of the Emirate of Dubai
